tool_args.label: MTA Board Action Items December 2021
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Board Action Items - December 2021 including MTA 2022 Temporary Fare Promotions, Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign, procurements, budget adoption, and other business. MTA Board Meeting at 2 Broadway 20th Floor Board Room New York, NY 10004 Wednesday, 12/15/2021 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM ET.

payload.title: MTA Board Action Items

tool_args.label: Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: Implement the Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign for local, limited, and SBS bus service. The redesigned bus network will better serve ridership demand while improving customers' commutes and overall service reliability via customer focused routes, and faster and more reliable travel times.

payload.description: Implementation of Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign for local, limited, and SBS bus service. Includes alignment changes to 16 regular bus routes including two new routes (Bx25 and M125), bus stop removals (~400 stops), frequency changes, and expanded bus priority on ten key corridors.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The proposed Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign focuses on reimagining New York City's bus network to provide customer-focused routes, and faster and more reliable travel times.
Implement the Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign for local, limited, and SBS bus service.
alignment changes to 16 regular bus routes, two being new routes

tool_args.label: Estimated monthly revenue loss from railroad promotions
tool_args.raw_text: MTA staff estimates that the three railroad promotions could cost the MTA approximately $3 million in lost revenue per month.

payload.raw_value_text: approximately $3 million in lost revenue per month
payload.description: Estimated lost revenue from the three proposed temporary railroad fare promotions (20-trip ticket, additional 10% monthly discount, City Ticket expansion)

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA staff estimates that the three railroad promotions could cost the MTA approximately $3 million in lost revenue per month.

tool_args.label: Estimated monthly revenue loss from OMNY Best Fare feature
tool_args.raw_text: The introduction of the 7-day best fare feature could cost the MTA between $3 million and $5 million in lost revenue per month.

payload.raw_value_text: between $3 million and $5 million in lost revenue per month
payload.description: Estimated lost revenue from the proposed OMNY Weekly Best Fare feature for subway, local bus and SIR customers

evidence_refs.source_quote: The introduction of the 7-day best fare feature could cost the MTA between $3 million and $5 million in lost revenue per month.

tool_args.label: Bronx Local Bus Redesign net annual operating cost increase
tool_args.raw_text: The Bronx Local Bus Redesign will increase the net annual operating cost of Bronx bus service by approximately $2.7 million.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Bronx Local Bus Redesign will increase the net annual operating cost of Bronx bus service by approximately $2.7 million.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Implementation of most routing and schedule changes is proposed for summer 2022

tool_args.label: MTA 2022 Temporary Fare Promotions introduction date
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA expects to introduce these promotions on February 25, 2022 (in time for the pre-sale of monthly tickets) to coincide with the restoration of regular peak fares.

payload.description: Introduction of temporary fare promotions for railroads (20-trip ticket, additional monthly discount, City Ticket expansion)

evidence_refs.source_quote: The MTA expects to introduce these promotions on February 25, 2022

tool_args.label: OMNY Best Fare feature introduction - Spring 2022
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA expects to introduce the OMNY best fare feature in Spring 2022.

payload.description: Introduction of OMNY Weekly Best Fare feature for subway, local bus and SIR customers

evidence_refs.source_quote: The MTA expects to introduce the OMNY best fare feature in Spring 2022.

tool_args.label: Bx6 SBS realignment delayed to 2023
tool_args.raw_text: Due to the 18-month pause caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, Bx6 SBS implementation will be delayed until 2023 to coincide with the retirement of MetroCard, the full deployment of OMNY across the city, and the implementation of all-door boarding in 2023.

payload.description: Bx6 SBS realignment to Story Avenue corridor delayed to 2023

evidence_refs.source_quote: Due to the 18-month pause caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, Bx6 SBS implementation will be delayed until 2023

tool_args.label: Purpose of 2022 Temporary Fare Promotions
tool_args.raw_text: The primary goals of these promotions are to: respond to post-pandemic travel patterns and attract customers now traveling less frequently than they were pre-pandemic; incentivize frequent customers to purchase ticket products that best suit their needs; expand customer adoption of OMNY and mobile ticketing; protect the finances of the MTA; and evaluate the impact of these promotions on operations.

payload.claim_text: The primary goals of these promotions are to: respond to post-pandemic travel patterns and attract customers now traveling less frequently than they were pre-pandemic; incentivize frequent customers to purchase ticket products that best suit their needs; expand customer adoption of OMNY and mobile ticketing; protect the finances of the MTA; and evaluate the impact of these promotions on operations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The primary goals of these promotions are to: respond to post-pandemic travel patterns and attract customers now traveling less frequently than they were pre-pandemic

tool_args.label: OMNY share of farebox revenue growth
tool_args.raw_text: OMNY was launched in May 2019, and after only 30 months the OMNY share of total subway/bus farebox revenue has grown to 19%.

payload.claim_text: OMNY was launched in May 2019, and after only 30 months the OMNY share of total subway/bus farebox revenue has grown to 19%.

evidence_refs.source_quote: OMNY was launched in May 2019, and after only 30 months the OMNY share of total subway/bus farebox revenue has grown to 19%.

tool_args.label: MTA eTix share of ticket sales growth
tool_args.raw_text: At the railroads, MTA eTix purchases now make up 50% of all ticket sales, up from 40% in 2019.

payload.claim_text: At the railroads, MTA eTix purchases now make up 50% of all ticket sales, up from 40% in 2019.

evidence_refs.source_quote: At the railroads, MTA eTix purchases now make up 50% of all ticket sales, up from 40% in 2019.

tool_args.label: Bronx redesign Title VI equity analysis finding
tool_args.raw_text: A service equity analysis, conducted in accordance with Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and related Federal Transit Administration guidance materials, found that the Bronx Bus Network Redesign proposal would not result in a disproportionate impact on either minority or below-poverty populations.

payload.claim_text: found that the Bronx Bus Network Redesign proposal would not result in a disproportionate impact on either minority or below-poverty populations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: A service equity analysis, conducted in accordance with Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ... found that the Bronx Bus Network Redesign proposal would not result in a disproportionate impact on either minority or below-poverty populations.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: M125 - new route
tool_args.raw_text: The M125 is a new route being proposed with changes to the Bx15 and M100. The Bx15 will be shortened, terminating at The Hub, with the new M125 operating from The Hub to 125th Street in Manhattan, terminating in Manhattanville.

payload.description: New route from The Hub to Manhattanville via 125th Street. Created from split of Bx15. Operates at weekday frequency of 8 minutes or better all-day with 24-hour service span.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The M125 is a new route being proposed with changes to the Bx15 and M100.
Operate route at weekday frequency of 8 minutes or better all-day with same service span as Bx15 (24 hours)

tool_args.label: Bx25 - new route
tool_args.raw_text: The Bx25 will provide service from northern Co-op City to Allerton Avenue and Bedford Park. It will share the Allerton Avenue corridor with the Bx26

payload.description: New route from northern Co-op City to Allerton Avenue and Bedford Park. Shares Allerton Avenue corridor with Bx26.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Bx25 will provide service from northern Co-op City to Allerton Avenue and Bedford Park.
